calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-04-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-04-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Appoint Yvonne Cota As Successor-In-Interest To Deceased Plaintiff David Cota
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Advanced From Jan-13-26 To Dec-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

Motion To Appoint Yvonne Cota As Successor-In-Interest To Deceased Plaintiff David Cota
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DAVID COTA'S Motion To Appoint Yvonne Cota As Successor-In-Interest To Deceased Plaintiff David Cota.

The motion to appoint Yvonne Cota as successor in-interest to deceased plaintiff David Cota is granted. Defendant informed the court at the court at the ex parte hearing that it does not oppose the motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-20-2025 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

PLAINTIFF DAVID COTA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
Off Calendar Jan-31-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Jan-31-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. OLGA PONCE, Esq. of Quill & Arrow (tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiff.
Atty. KENNETH HOLLENBECK, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (tel: 415-875-3193) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DAVID COTA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

Plaintiff David Cota's motion to compel further responses to Plaintiff's first set of RFPs is granted in part and denied in part.

With respect to request nos. 1, 3-11 and 13-14, these requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Cota has satisfied his burden of setting forth specific facts showing good cause for production. (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) These requests relate to the pickup truck at issue in this case. As a general matter, where Ford responds with objections and then a statement of selected documents it will produce, it does not comply with its obligations under CCP 2031.240 to identify documents that are the subject of the objection and withheld on that basis.

With respect to request 12, Ford's response was adequate and no further response is ordered. Moreover, as to RFP 2, no further amendments or responses are required to be produced.

With respect to request nos. 15-31, these requests relate to Cota's allegation that Ford's violation of the Song-Beverly Act was willful and that Cota is entitled to civil penalties. The relevance of documents produced depends on Cota's showing that Ford violated the Song-Beverly Act. Ford objected on breadth and burdens grounds but offered to produce specified documents and categories of documents. In a supplemental response that postdated Cota's motion to compel, Ford offered to produce documents related to other customers' complaints about the same model of truck that Cota owned. Cota's motion to compel makes broad arguments about the scope of potentially admissible material in a Song-Beverly case but does not set forth specific facts justifying further production of documents with reference to, for example, what is missing from the documents Ford has already produced or offered to produce. Cota has not satisfied his burden at this juncture in the case of setting forth specific facts showing good cause for production. (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) The motion is denied as to these requests without prejudice to Cota's service of RFPs concerning these topics after Ford has completed its initial production.

Ford is ordered to amend its responses to request nos 1-11 and 13-14 within 30 days of the date of this order. Ford shall provide a verification. Within 30 days of the date of this order, Ford is ordered to complete its production of all responsive documents, and any documents produced in response to request nos. 15-31, other than documents withheld on grounds of privilege, for which a privilege log shall be produced. For protected documents, production must be done within 30 days of entry of protective order.

The court finds that Cota's meet and confer efforts were reasonable.

Counsel Olga Ponce is to prepare the order which repeats the ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2025 Set For Trial On Dec-15-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
